name: eval-2-chained-from-prompt-architect
context_setup: Previous turns: user invoked prompt-architect, ran through Phase 1 discovery, got a generated prompt for auditing PR descriptions, tested it on a sample PR and confirmed it works. The generated prompt is already in chat context with all variables and structure.
query: This is exactly what I wanted. Install it.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this as the chain trigger from prompt-architect ('this is exactly what I wanted')
Skips redundant Phase 1 questions — name, description, triggers, prompt logic, and output format are already established from the prompt-architect session
ONLY asks the questions that prompt-architect did not already answer (probably just: confirm the kebab-case name and ask about auto-detect triggers if not specified)
Builds the SKILL.md using the prompt-architect output as the source of truth, restructured into proper SKILL.md format
Updates README and pushes via container deploy fallback
Does NOT re-interrogate the user about things they already established two turns ago
Surfaces the new SKILL.md before pushing so the user can sanity-check
failure_modes_to_check: Re-runs full Phase 1 discovery even though prompt-architect already covered it
Loses fidelity from the prompt-architect output (drops examples, variables, or structure)
Pushes without showing the user the proposed SKILL.md first
